gpu: AMD Radeon RX 5700 XT (navi10, LLVM 16.0.6, DRM 3.54, 6.5.5-arch1-1)
notes:
There were times where I was able to play for a whole 5 to 10 minutes, but it would crash all the same. I even looked through my syslog, and nothing seems out of the ordinary, just an ordinary crash because of my drivers not working with CS2 _specifically_. I literally have no other issues with any other game, and my computer works just fine. This is a specific problem with the native linux version not working. I would try to make the proton version work, but it won't let me join normal matchmaking because of VAC. 

Valve. Please fix your game. Literally all the linux nerds in their basements, who do free playtesting and bug report out of pure love for the game,  are giving you literally _invaluable_ feedback to make the game better, for windows users or any other user for that matter. And yet they still have it the hardest when it comes to just simply be able to _play_ the game. I am hoping that in the next year, hopefully, I'll get to play normally, without needing to tinker anything. This whole experience has been just frustrating. 


I have tried both in wayland and in Xorg, and in both, I have tried it with the "-vulkan -thread 12" setup to make it work, as well as without any tinkering. In all four instances, as well as in many variations of these (Only one monitor, with both monitors, with different drivers, with older version of the drivers, etc.), I couldn't make it work more than 5 to 10 minutes at a time, before it just crashed.
title: Counter-Strike 2

notes:
The game launches, albeit very lagged. Going into the settings menu and trying to start a match crashes the game. Steam then proceeds to download 2GB of something for the game every time it crashes. Further attempts yield same results.
Launching the game using Proton seems to make it more stable and playable, but doesn't recognize any audio output devices, therefore making the game mute.
